rubric.evidence: Headless-Chrome full flow (fresh profile): first visit shows loader state=download-required with a Download button (NO auto-download); after Download, state=ready; the sample clip analysed to 'Speech detected — 4 segments' in 83 ms with backend 'wasm (onnxruntime-web)'. Console + network clean. Standalone runnability probe (onnxruntime-web 1.20.1, model.onnx): silence-window avg prob 0.009 vs speech-window avg prob 0.978, stateful LSTM carried across 512-sample frames. Screens: reports/responsive/screens/silero-vad/desktop.png, mobile.png.
rubric.notes: Silero VAD has NO transformers.js pipeline; inference is driven with onnxruntime-web directly, isolated to models/silero-vad/worker.js (version-pin/custom-worker precedent). Shared lib/webai.js untouched.

rubric.evidence: Output is a per-frame speech probability [0,1] per 512-sample (32 ms) frame; the page renders the real probability curve + threshold + hysteresis-derived speech segments. On the real jfk speech clip it produced 4 plausible segments; on the synthetic music/ambient clips it reports no speech (correctly, an energy-heavy chord is non-speech). Full-flow run confirmed segment count + latency on-page.
rubric.notes: voice-activity-detection is a new task (registered via the models.json entry; index.html renders it dynamically).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/silero-vad, linked), task (voice-activity-detection), runtime (onnxruntime-web), dtype (fp32), and backend (WASM). Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker (models/silero-vad/worker.js). Conformance runner: declares-model-id / declares-task / declares-backend / declares-quantisation / inference-off-main-thread all auto-checkable.

Uses the SHARED createModelLoader auto-init architecture. The worker stores the ~2 MB model in Cache Storage under its real HF URL so lib/model-cache.js detects it. Full flow confirmed: after first download, a page reload AUTO-INITIALISED to state=ready with NO Download button and a clean console (reloadAutoInit=true, reloadHadDownloadBtn=false).

Loader announces checking → downloading (with a progress bar wired to the worker's byte-progress) → ready via role=status aria-live. Failures surface on the page (.status.err) with a Retry path. Backend is WASM only (no WebGPU requirement) so it runs everywhere; no blank/faked state observed. Console + network clean across first visit and reload.

Every control exercised: sample chips (aria-pressed), file dropzone (role=button, Enter/Space), mic record, threshold + min-silence range sliders (labelled, with <output> live values), Analyse button. The threshold/min-silence sliders re-derive segments client-side without re-running inference. Manual keyboard/focus verdict on both device classes still pending an agent matrix read.
rubric.notes: auto-checkable parts pass; keyboard-focus/tap-target verdict is manual-evidenced.

rubric.evidence: The verdict, curve, timeline, segment table, and readout are all derived from the real per-frame probabilities returned by onnxruntime-web — there is no canned path. When no speech is found the page says so honestly.

Buttons have accessible names; canvases have descriptive aria-labels; status is a live region; range sliders are label-associated with visible <output> values. Conformance images-have-alt / buttons-have-names auto-checks pass. Full SR pass on both classes pending an agent read of the screenshots.

Responsive matrix (automated): desktop 1280×800 and mobile 360×740 DPR3 — no horizontal overflow (overflow 0, 0 clipped controls) on either class. Layout uses flex-wrap + a min(100%,220px) auto-fit param grid with min-inline-size:0 (css-layout guidance). Screens: reports/responsive/screens/silero-vad/desktop.png, mobile.png. Sub-24px note flags small range-slider thumbs to verify against tap-target guidance.
rubric.notes: auto-checkable overflow/clipping pass; tap-target + legibility verdict is manual-evidenced.

rubric.evidence: All inference is off the main thread (onnxruntime-web in a Web Worker); the frame loop and the live-mic streaming path both run in the worker, so the control UI stays responsive (identify-inp-causes guidance). Analyse latency on the sample clip was 83 ms. Curve/segment re-derivation on a threshold change is a light client-side pass, no re-inference.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing (min(100%,220px) auto-fit tracks, min-inline-size:0) instead of fixed widths so control panels never overflow narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the parameter grid, sample row, field rows, and readouts all wrap; range sliders set inline-size:100%;min-inline-size:0. Responsive matrix confirms no horizontal overflow at 360px or 1280px.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep interactions responsive by moving heavy JavaScript off the interaction path.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the entire VAD frame loop (and the live-mic streaming path) runs in a Web Worker via onnxruntime-web; the main thread only draws. Parameter changes re-derive segments with a light client-side pass, not re-inference.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js runs onnxruntime-web off-thread; conformance inference-off-main-thread = pass; 83 ms analyse latency

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state in a live region; associate every control with a visible label and expose its current value.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — loader + page status use role=status aria-live; range sliders are <label for> associated with a live <output>; canvases carry descriptive aria-labels.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance buttons-have-names / live-status-region = pass; full-flow status transitions observed

openQuestions: On a real touch device, are the threshold / min-silence slider thumbs comfortable to drag (the automated scan flagged sub-24px targets)?
Does the live-mic talk-meter (wild) stay smooth on a low-end mobile CPU running the 32 ms frame loop in real time?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for silero-vad: Read the mobile+desktop screenshots, confirm slider tap-targets + focus order + the live talk-meter on a touch device, then flip support to ok (or unsupported+evidence).
Consider an AudioWorklet upgrade for the live-mic path (currently ScriptProcessorNode) to reduce audio-thread overhead on mobile.

summary: Revision-1 critique for silero-vad (voice-activity-detection via onnxruntime-web). Real in-browser inference is confirmed end-to-end (fresh Download → 4 speech segments on a real clip → reload auto-init, console/network clean). Auto-checkable dimensions pass; tap-target/focus and live-mic-on-mobile verdicts await an agent matrix read.
